expectations.text: Treats this correctly as the agency's incident too: activate the agency IR plan, establish what agency data/classification the supplier holds, and demand supplier containment/forensics status under the contract
expectations.evidence: 'Activate your incident response plan ... Contain and assess ... The classification of the data involved ... Preserve evidence ... Review your contract with the supplier ... invoke these immediately.'

expectations.text: Names the right NZ notification channel for cyber incidents: report to the NCSC (GCSB) — noting CERT NZ's functions are now within the NCSC — plus NZ Police for the criminal act where appropriate
expectations.evidence: Lists NCSC and CERT NZ as two separate, still-active channels: 'report the incident to whichever of NCSC/CERT NZ is the current designated channel for government agency incidents' and checklist items 'NCSC (GCSB) notified' and 'CERT NZ notified (or current equivalent...)' as distinct entries, plus a closing note saying the 'division of responsibilities between NCSC and CERT NZ should be verified.' This hedges/conflates rather than correctly stating that CERT NZ's functions have been absorbed into the NCSC, contradicting the ground truth in the assertion.

expectations.text: Identifies the Privacy Act 2020 obligation: if personal information is involved and the breach is notifiable (serious harm threshold), notify the Office of the Privacy Commissioner and affected individuals
expectations.evidence: 'Office of the Privacy Commissioner ... New Zealand's mandatory breach notification regime requires notification to: The Privacy Commissioner, and Affected individuals ... where the breach has caused, or is likely to cause, serious harm.'

expectations.text: Covers evidence preservation and reporting discipline: preserve logs/artifacts, document a timeline, and conduct a post-incident review feeding lessons back into supplier controls
expectations.evidence: 'Preserve evidence — ensure the supplier preserves logs and forensic evidence' and 'Timeline of the incident and when the supplier became aware'; Section 5 'Formal post-incident review ... document root cause, supplier's response adequacy ... Reassess the supplier relationship.'

expectations.text: Addresses the supplier-contract dimension: invoke incident-notification clauses, require remediation evidence, reassess the supplier's certification/assurance status, and update the agency risk register
expectations.evidence: 'Review your contract with the supplier for its incident notification and cooperation obligations ... invoke these immediately' and 'Reassess the supplier relationship — determine whether the supplier's security controls met contractual obligations ... Update your Security Risk Management Plan and vendor risk assessments.'
